source: Learning bash with Rocky - Check Your Knowledge (chapters 5-7): The book's own review quiz for tests, conditionals and loops

questions.q: Every command returns a return code at the end of its execution. True or false?
questions.why: All commands return a status/return code on completion.

questions.q: A return code of 0 indicates an execution error. True or false?
questions.why: 0 means success; a non-zero code indicates an error.

questions.q: The return code is stored in the variable $@. True or false?
questions.why: The return code is stored in $?, not $@ (which holds the arguments).

questions.q: Which of these can the test command do?
options: All of these: test a file's type, test a variable, compare numbers, compare two files
questions.why: test works on files (type/rights/comparison), strings, and integers.

questions.q: Is this if syntax correct?  if command / command if $?=0 / else / command if $?!=0 / fi
questions.why: It is missing the `then` keyword after the if command.

options: Assigns a new value to the variable if it is empty
Displays a replacement value if the variable is empty
Displays a replacement value if the variable is not empty
questions.why: := assigns the value when the variable is empty.

questions.q: Is this case syntax correct (branches value1) and value2) with no ;; before *)?
questions.why: Each branch's commands must be terminated by ;;; the first two branches are missing it.

questions.q: Which of these is NOT a looping structure?
questions.why: while, until, for (and select) are loops; there is no `loop` keyword.
